title: Makefile: Add stderr redirect to HAS_PYTHON3 definition
body:
For two reasons:

* When a system is missing `python3`, we don't need to spam them with "Command not found" in their stderr.

* Without the redirect, GNU Make (at least version 4.2.1) is overly clever and tries to invoke the command itself, [not realizing that it's a shell builtin][1]:

    ```console
    $ make --version
    GNU Make 4.2.1
    Built for aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
    Copyright (C) 1988-2016 Free Software Foundation, Inc.
    License GPLv3+: GNU GPL version 3 or later <http://gnu.org/licenses/gpl.html>
    This is free software: you are free to change and redistribute it.
    There is NO WARRANTY, to the extent permitted by law.
    $ cat Makefile
    PYTHON3_A := $(shell command -v python3)
    PYTHON3_B := $(shell command -v python3 2>/dev/null)

    test:
            @echo "SHELL: '$(SHELL)'"
            @echo "PYTHON3_A: '$(PYTHON3_A)'"
            @echo "PYTHON3_B: '$(PYTHON3_B)'"
    $ make
    make: command: Command not found
    SHELL: '/bin/sh'
    PYTHON3_A: ''
    PYTHON3_B: '/usr/bin/python3'
    ```

    By adding the redirect we actually hit the shell and can successfully invoke `command`.

[1]: https://stackoverflow.com/a/17550243

comments.body:
Checking Travis, here's [an example of a previous run][1] exhibiting the "make: command: Command not found" issue and then not building the Python package.  And here's Travis with this commit, [successfully building the Python package][2].

[1]: https://travis-ci.org/projectatomic/libpod/jobs/385791381#L851
[2]: https://travis-ci.org/projectatomic/libpod/jobs/385904691#L853

`cloud Podman` is complaining about [some Python failures][1]:

```
+ python3 -m unittest discover -s .
...........................F.EE.......
======================================================================
ERROR: test_tag (test.test_images.TestImages)
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/contrib/python/test/test_images.py", line 81, in test_tag
    self.assertEqual(self.alpine_image.id,
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'id'

======================================================================
ERROR: tearDownClass (test.test_images.TestImages)
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/contrib/python/test/test_images.py", line 16, in tearDownClass
    super().tearDownClass()
  File "/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/contrib/python/test/podman_testcase.py", line 94, in tearDownClass
    assert 0 == PodmanTestCase.alpine_process.wait(500)
AssertionError

======================================================================
FAIL: test_remove (test.test_images.TestImages)
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/contrib/python/test/test_images.py", line 98, in test_remove
    TestImages.setUpClass()
  File "/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/contrib/python/test/test_images.py", line 12, in setUpClass
    super().setUpClass()
  File "/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/contrib/python/test/podman_testcase.py", line 20, in setUpClass
    PodmanTestCase.tearDownClass()
  File "/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/contrib/python/test/podman_testcase.py", line 94, in tearDownClass
    assert 0 == PodmanTestCase.alpine_process.wait(500)
AssertionError

----------------------------------------------------------------------
Ran 37 tests in 221.457s

FAILED (failures=1, errors=2)
```

I'm not sure what's going on there.  @baude, @jwhonce, ideas?

[1]: https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/2a906499d188a8c6a32f3679b5f9e6e503bbeb09.2.1527715944752616008/output.log
@wking I saw that elsewhere today on an unrelated PR, I think this is just infrastructure-related flakes

We are moving in the wrong direction, with [three errors now][1] ;).  Should we make them nonfatal until the flakes are addressed?

[1]: https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/2a906499d188a8c6a32f3679b5f9e6e503bbeb09.2.1527721756430711903/output.log

@mheon @wking @baude Yup, the tests pull from docker.io and they're failing with: 

varlink.error.VarlinkError: {'parameters': {'reason': 'unable to pull prom/busybox:latest: unable to pull prom/busybox:latest: error pulling image from: Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://prom/busybox:latest: pinging docker registry returned: Get https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/: dial tcp: i/o timeout'}, 'error': 'io.projectatomic.podman.ErrorOccurred'}

Similar errors on [this recent run][1].  Are we sure these aren't recent libpod issues?  I'd be surprised if Docket's registry was this unreliable.

[1]: https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/2a906499d188a8c6a32f3679b5f9e6e503bbeb09.2.1527726131927330369/output.log
@wking I think it's the cluster hosting the tests, as opposed to the registry. We've had long periods of the tests just not working at all, though things stabilized a lot since around December.

> I think it's the cluster hosting the tests...

Would it help to run a local registry of some sort?  As far as libpod is concerned, I expect we can leave Docker-registry pulls to container/image (or wherever it's implemented) and just use the simplest transport for our CI.

:pushpin: Commit 2a90649 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 2a906499d188a8c6a32f3679b5f9e6e503bbeb09 -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
